text.title: tree-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tree-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated tasks copy destination node labels verbatim into scenario text.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tasks generated by agents almost always leak the destination label. The leak-check pass is mandatory for all agent-generated tasks, not optional. Run it before any human review.

failure-mode.description: Including "decoy" tasks where the right answer does not exist in the tree, revealing whether participants abandon or guess confidently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is actually a best practice, not an antipattern. Include 2-3 decoy tasks. They expose false-confidence behavior: participants who click confidently to wrong locations even when the correct answer is absent need a different IA diagnosis than those who abandon.

failure-mode.description: Using synthetic LLM "participants" to substitute for real participant data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs roleplay tree tests by applying their own semantic priors about label relationships, not naive-user mental models. Useful only for sanity-checking task quality — never as data. Real participants are required.

failure-mode.description: Recruiting from cheap general consumer panels that inflate success rates because professional respondents click confidently and randomly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Professional respondents who cycle through research studies produce falsely high success rates — they click confidently anywhere. Screen for category familiarity matching real users of the product.

failure-mode.description: Aggregating across tasks into a single "site success rate" number without per-task breakdown.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overall site success is a reporting artifact. The decision input is per-task metrics with worst tasks highlighted. An average of 73% can hide three tasks at 30% success that each require immediate IA work.

failure-mode.description: Assuming Treejack, Maze, and UXtweak CSV exports share the same column schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each tool uses different column names and success-category labels. Build provider-specific adapter functions for metric computation rather than assuming a shared schema.
